title: Authors and Citation
text: Hodge D (2026). ggwidth: Standardise 'ggplot2' Geom Width. R package version 0.1.0.9000, https://github.com/davidhodge931/ggwidth.
code: @Manual{,   title = {ggwidth: Standardise 'ggplot2' Geom Width},   author = {David Hodge},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0.9000},   url = {https://github.com/davidhodge931/ggwidth}, }

title: Standardise ggplot2 Geom Width
text: objective ggwidth standardise ‘ggplot2’ geom width. provides methods ensure width ggplot2 geoms visually consistent across plots different numbers categories, panel dimensions, orientations. works geoms geom_bar/geom_col, geom_boxplot geom_errorbar. Note function requires: set theme panel widths heights specified "x" orientation plots x discrete scale default expand "y" orientation plots y discrete scale default expand.

title: Standardise ggplot2 Geom Width
code: library(ggplot2) library(dplyr) library(ggwidth) library(patchwork)  set_theme(   theme_grey() +     theme(panel.widths  = rep(unit(75, "mm"), 2)) +     theme(panel.heights = rep(unit(50, "mm"), 2)) )  set_equiwidth(1) p1 <- mpg |>   ggplot(aes(x = drv)) +   geom_bar(     width = get_width(n = 3),     colour = "black",     fill = "grey",   )  p2 <- diamonds |>   ggplot(aes(x = color)) +   geom_bar(     width = get_width(n = 7),     colour = "black",     fill = "grey",   )  p3 <- diamonds |>   ggplot(aes(y = color)) +   geom_bar(     width = get_width(n = 7, orientation = "y"),     colour = "black",     fill = "grey",   )  p4 <- mpg |>   ggplot(aes(x = drv, group = factor(cyl))) +   geom_bar(     position = position_dodge(preserve = "single"),     width = get_width(n = 3, n_dodge = 4),     colour = "black",     fill = "grey",   )   p1 + p2 + p3 + p4 d <- tibble::tibble(   continent = c("Europe", "Europe", "Europe", "Europe", "Europe",                 "South America", "South America"),   country   = c("AT", "DE", "DK", "ES", "PK", "TW", "BR"),   value     = c(10L, 15L, 20L, 25L, 17L, 13L, 5L) )  max_n <- d |>   count(continent) |>   pull(n) |>   max()  d |>   mutate(country = forcats::fct_rev(country)) |>   ggplot(aes(y = country, x = value)) +   geom_col(     width = get_width(n = max_n, orientation = "y"),     colour = "black",     fill = "grey",   ) +   facet_wrap(~continent, scales = "free_y") +   scale_y_discrete(continuous.limits = c(1, max_n)) +   coord_cartesian(reverse = "y", clip = "off") mpg |>   ggplot(aes(x = drv)) +   geom_bar(     width = get_width(n = 3, panel_widths = unit(160, "mm")),     colour = "black",     fill = "grey",   ) +   theme(panel.widths = unit(160, "mm"))

title: Standardise 'ggplot2' geom width — get_width
text: Standardise width 'ggplot2' geoms appear visually consistent across plots different numbers categories, panel dimensions, orientations. can used geoms ggplot2::geom_bar()/ggplot2::geom_col(), ggplot2::geom_boxplot(), ggplot2::geom_errorbar().

title: Standardise 'ggplot2' geom width — get_width
code: get_width(   ...,   n = NULL,   n_dodge = NULL,   orientation = c("x", "y"),   equiwidth = NULL,   panel_widths = NULL,   panel_heights = NULL )

title: Standardise 'ggplot2' geom width — get_width
text: ... Must empty. Forces arguments named allows trailing commas. n Number categories orientation aesthetic (.e. "x" "y"). faceted plots, use maximum n within facet. n_dodge Number dodge categories. Must match number groups fill colour aesthetic using position_dodge(). orientation Orientation: "x" vertical (width appearance equiwidth panel width), "y" horizontal (width appearance equiwidth panel height). equiwidth Numeric. Scaling factor controls width appearance. value 1 default. Increase make wider appearance, decrease make thinner appearance. NULL, uses value set set_equiwidth(), falling back 1. panel_widths grid::unit object specifying panel width. NULL (default), uses value set current theme. panel_heights grid::unit object specifying panel height. NULL (default), uses value set current theme.

title: Standardise 'ggplot2' geom width — get_width
text: numeric width value passed width argument geom_bar(), geom_col(), similar geoms.

title: Standardise 'ggplot2' geom width — get_width
code: library(ggplot2) library(dplyr) #>  #> Attaching package: ‘dplyr’ #> The following objects are masked from ‘package:stats’: #>  #>     filter, lag #> The following objects are masked from ‘package:base’: #>  #>     intersect, setdiff, setequal, union library(patchwork)  set_theme(   theme_grey() +     theme(panel.widths  = rep(unit(75, "mm"), 2)) +     theme(panel.heights = rep(unit(50, "mm"), 2)) ) set_equiwidth(1)  p1 <- mpg |>   ggplot(aes(x = drv)) +   geom_bar(     width = get_width(n = 3),     colour = "black",     fill = "grey",   )  p2 <- diamonds |>   ggplot(aes(x = color)) +   geom_bar(     width = get_width(n = 7),     colour = "black",     fill = "grey",   )  p3 <- diamonds |>   ggplot(aes(y = color)) +   geom_bar(     width = get_width(n = 7, orientation = "y"),     colour = "black",     fill = "grey",   )  p4 <- mpg |>   ggplot(aes(x = drv, group = factor(cyl))) +   geom_bar(     position = position_dodge(preserve = "single"),     width = get_width(n = 3, n_dodge = 4),     colour = "black",     fill = "grey",   )  p1 + p2 + p3 + p4   d <- tibble::tibble(   continent = c("Europe", "Europe", "Europe", "Europe", "Europe",                 "South America", "South America"),   country   = c("AT", "DE", "DK", "ES", "PK", "TW", "BR"),   value     = c(10L, 15L, 20L, 25L, 17L, 13L, 5L) )  max_n <- d |>   count(continent) |>   pull(n) |>   max()  d |>   mutate(country = forcats::fct_rev(country)) |>   ggplot(aes(y = country, x = value)) +   geom_col(     width = get_width(n = max_n, orientation = "y"),     colour = "black",     fill = "grey",   ) +   facet_wrap(~continent, scales = "free_y") +   scale_y_discrete(continuous.limits = c(1, max_n)) +   coord_cartesian(reverse = "y", clip = "off")   mpg |>   ggplot(aes(x = drv)) +   geom_bar(     width = get_width(n = 3, panel_widths = unit(160, "mm")),     colour = "black",     fill = "grey",   ) +   theme(panel.widths = unit(160, "mm"))

title: ggwidth: Standardise 'ggplot2' Geom Width — ggwidth-package
text: Standardise width 'ggplot2' geoms appear visually consistent across plots different numbers categories, panel dimensions, orientations.

title: Set a global equiwidth — set_equiwidth
text: Sets global default equiwidth argument get_width(). scaling factor controls width appearance. subsequent calls get_width() use value equiwidth = NULL, fall back 1.

title: Set a global equiwidth — set_equiwidth
text: equiwidth Numeric. Multiplicative factor controls width appearance. value 1 default. Increase make wider appearance, decrease make thinner appearance.
